ports.name: Nassau
ports.description: Pirate haven in the Bahamas — loose governance, high traffic

ports.name: Tortuga
ports.description: Notorious pirate refuge off Hispaniola's northern coast

ports.name: Havana
ports.description: Spanish crown jewel — gateway to the Gulf of Mexico

ports.name: Port Royal
ports.description: British stronghold on Jamaica's southern coast

ports.name: Santiago
ports.description: Spanish port on Cuba's eastern tip, guards the passage

ports.name: Bridgetown
ports.description: British sugar colony in the Windward Islands

ports.name: Cartagena
ports.description: Fortified Spanish treasure port on the Spanish Main

ports.name: Maracaibo
ports.description: Spanish colonial port on the Venezuelan coast

ports.name: Panama City
ports.description: Pacific gateway — Spanish silver route terminus

ports.name: Santo Domingo
ports.description: First permanent European settlement in the Americas

ports.name: Martinique
ports.description: French sugar island in the eastern Caribbean

ports.name: Trinidad
ports.description: British outpost near the Venezuelan delta

ports.name: Kingston
ports.description: Growing British naval base, rivals Port Royal

ports.name: Bermuda
ports.description: Remote British outpost — resupply stop for Atlantic crossings

ports.name: Curacao
ports.description: Dutch trading hub — neutral market for contraband and goods

ports.name: Saint Kitts
ports.description: First permanent British settlement in the Caribbean

trade_routes.name: Spanish Main Route
trade_routes.description: Core Spanish colonial trade artery — gold, silver, and tobacco flow between Cuba and the Main

trade_routes.name: British Sugar Run
trade_routes.description: British inter-island sugar and rum trade between Jamaica and Barbados

trade_routes.name: Silver Galleon Path
trade_routes.description: Most valuable route — Spanish treasure fleets carrying New World silver

trade_routes.name: Dutch Contraband Loop
trade_routes.description: Dutch smugglers trade manufactured goods for Venezuelan cacao and hides

trade_routes.name: Pirate Brotherhood Run
trade_routes.description: Pirate supply and intelligence exchange between Bahamian and Haitian bases

trade_routes.name: French-Spanish Border Trade
trade_routes.description: Uneasy commerce across the French-Spanish island border

chokepoints.name: Windward Passage
chokepoints.description: Narrow strait between Cuba and Hispaniola — controls eastern Caribbean access. All east-west traffic must pass through here.

chokepoints.name: Yucatan Channel
chokepoints.description: Gateway into the Gulf of Mexico between Cuba and the Yucatan Peninsula. Havana's defensive buffer.

chokepoints.name: Mona Passage
chokepoints.description: Tight passage between Hispaniola and Puerto Rico — controls access to the Leeward Islands from the north.

chokepoints.name: Venezuela Narrows
chokepoints.description: Coastal pinch-point along the Venezuelan coast between Curacao and Trinidad — key for controlling Main access.

safe_zones.description: Spanish Armada's primary patrol zone — three warships guard the approaches at all times
Royal Navy patrol out of Port Royal — heavily enforced, fastest British response time
Cartagena fortress zone — fortified walls extend influence far out to sea
British colonial patrol — lighter than Port Royal, but reinforced during sugar harvest season
Pirate Brotherhood's informal protection zone — outsiders enter at their own risk
